frontmatter.name: universal-task-execution-engine
frontmatter.description:
Executes structured tasks using deterministic workflows, validation, and measurable outputs.

Use when a user requests repeatable, multi-step, or operationally structured work.

Activation signals:
- Keywords: execute, process, run, perform, structured, workflow
- Intents: complete task, perform procedure, run process
- Context: multi-step request with defined outcome required

Exclusions: casual conversation, speculative reasoning without defined outcome.

Priority: 8 (high)

frontmatter.name: kiyosaki-financial-advisor
frontmatter.description: Financial mindset advisor based on Rich Dad Poor Dad principles. Use when users seek wealth-building strategies, asset vs liability analysis, passive income advice, financial education, or challenges to traditional financial thinking.

frontmatter.name: ctv-campaign-planner
frontmatter.description: Design, model, and optimize local Connected TV advertising campaigns for nonprofits, community initiatives, and mission-driven organizations. Use when the user requests CTV campaign planning, streaming TV advertising strategy, local awareness campaigns, nonprofit video ad strategy, media budget modeling, impression forecasting, campaign ROI estimation, audience targeting design, media funnel planning, or campaign launch planning.

frontmatter.name: paranoia-mode
frontmatter.description: "Paranoia Mode" — ensure every non-trivial answer or action is verified before being returned or executed. Never assume anything is true without explicit verification. If verification cannot be completed with high confidence, refuse to act autonomously in high-risk areas. Also handles toggling Paranoia Mode on/off via user commands.

frontmatter.name: module6-security-council
frontmatter.description: Module 6 Security Council for OpenClaw. Nightly multi-perspective security review with numbered findings, critical alerting, and Telegram delivery.

frontmatter.name: ctv-campaign-planner-pro
frontmatter.description: Design, configure, simulate, and justify Connected TV campaigns for local nonprofit and mission-driven organizations. Produces campaign strategy, media projections, DSP configuration settings, automatic Providence market modeling, video script generation, retargeting architecture, and grant funding justification. Use when user requests CTV campaign design, streaming TV media buying, Providence market campaigns, video ad creation, retargeting strategy, media platform configuration, campaign funding justification, or grant narrative support.

frontmatter.name: kiyosaki-wealth-advisor
frontmatter.description: Analyze financial queries and advise on wealth-building using Rich Dad Poor Dad principles. Use when users ask about wealth building, passive income, assets vs liabilities, financial independence, investing mindsets, buying a house, saving strategies, or employment vs entrepreneurship decisions.

frontmatter.name: module4-rag
frontmatter.description: Module 4 Knowledge Base (RAG) for OpenClaw. Ingest URLs from Telegram into SQLite with embeddings, entities, and semantic+time-aware retrieval.

frontmatter.name: context-compress
frontmatter.description: Compress OpenClaw session context to reduce token usage and maintain performance on long-running sessions. Use when sessions exceed token thresholds (e.g., 40k+ tokens), when conversation history becomes unwieldy, or when implementing automatic context compression in agent workflows. Supports manual compression triggers, automatic threshold-based compression, and backup preservation of original sessions.

frontmatter.name: betting-research-agent
frontmatter.description: AI agent for data-driven sports betting research and edge identification. This skill performs quantitative analysis to identify value bets, detect line discrepancies, track closing line value, and research market inefficiencies using professional bettor methodologies (Walters, Voulgaris, Monahan frameworks). Use whenever conducting betting research, analyzing odds for value, verifying statistical edges, tracking bet performance, comparing lines across sportsbooks, or building predictive models for sports betting. This is the primary tool for all analytical betting work in your workflow.

frontmatter.name: betting-research-agent
frontmatter.description: AI agent for data-driven sports betting research and edge identification. This skill performs quantitative analysis to identify value bets, detect line discrepancies, track closing line value, and research market inefficiencies using professional bettor methodologies (Walters, Voulgaris, Monahan frameworks). Use whenever conducting betting research, analyzing odds for value, verifying statistical edges, tracking bet performance, comparing lines across sportsbooks, or building predictive models for sports betting. This is the primary tool for all analytical betting work in your workflow.

frontmatter.name: openclaw-mission-control
frontmatter.description: Unified command center for OpenClaw operations. Real-time cost tracking, model management, sub-agent control, cron monitoring, and automated optimization. Use when monitoring OpenClaw session costs and cache efficiency, managing models and switching between local/remote, spawning or killing sub-agents, managing cron jobs and scheduled tasks, or analyzing usage patterns and optimizing spend.

frontmatter.name: content-extractor
frontmatter.description: Extract structured content from YouTube videos and web pages for AI processing. Use when an agent needs to pull text, transcripts, or metadata from URLs. Supports YouTube (video + transcript), web articles, and generic web pages. Outputs structured JSON with content, metadata, and source information.

frontmatter.name: social-media-community-manager
frontmatter.description: Social media strategy, content creation, and community management for Taste of Juneteenth New England (#TOJNE) — an annual celebration of African Diaspora food culture and Black-owned businesses on Juneteenth in Providence, RI. Use when planning content calendars, drafting posts, managing influencer collaborations, creating UGC workflows, developing community response templates, or building KPI frameworks for Instagram, Facebook, and TikTok.

frontmatter.name: verify-over-trust
frontmatter.description: Enforce "verify over trust, no exceptions" for all non-trivial answers and actions. Before returning any non-trivial answer or taking any action, verify claims through cross-checking, critique, or explicit logic verification. Classify risk as low (exploration), medium (personal/code), or high (money/safety/legal/irreversible). Never act autonomously in high-risk areas without human approval.

frontmatter.name: ai-agent-master-dev
frontmatter.description: Comprehensive framework for developing, integrating, and maintaining AI agent capabilities using the Agent Skills standard. Use when the user references agent development, skill integration, or tools like uvx, pipx, or deno run. Includes multi-persona support for system design, skill creation, DevOps deployment, and agent usage validation.

frontmatter.name: openrouter-cost-optimizer
frontmatter.description: Monitor OpenRouter API costs, optimize model selection based on task complexity and budget constraints, and generate daily spending reports. Use when managing model costs, selecting cost-effective models for tasks, setting up budget alerts, or analyzing spending patterns across different AI models.

frontmatter.name: paranoia-mode
frontmatter.description: Activate "Paranoia Mode" to ensure every non-trivial answer or action is verified before being returned or executed. Never assume anything is true without explicit verification. If verification cannot be completed with high confidence, refuse to act autonomously in high-risk areas. Supports toggling on/off via user commands.

frontmatter.description: Intelligent model selection based on task requirements, cost constraints, and capability matching. Use when selecting which AI model to use for a task, assessing whether to escalate to a more capable model, managing context window requirements, or optimizing cost vs. quality tradeoffs across local and remote models.

frontmatter.description: End-to-end recruiting support for hiring a Freelance Social Media & Community Manager for a U.S.-based home decor brand. Use when generating job posts, drafting candidate outreach, screening applicants, creating evaluation materials, or building onboarding plans for social media manager roles in e-commerce/DTC lifestyle brands.

Use when users ask about buying a house, saving money, getting promotions, passive income, financial independence, or mindset shifts around money. Challenges traditional "employee mindset" and shifts focus to asset acquisition and investing.

frontmatter.name: canvas-design
frontmatter.description: Create beautiful visual art in .png and .pdf documents using design philosophy. Use for creating posters, art, designs, or other static visuals.

frontmatter.name: product-ops-prompt-stack-2026
frontmatter.description: A modular 20-prompt framework for discovering, validating, building, pricing, launching, improving, and scaling digital products in 2026. Use when the user needs to find and validate product opportunities, create digital products with AI assistance, price and position products for sale, plan and execute product launches, scale and automate product businesses, or access proven product creation prompts and workflows.

frontmatter.name: zeroclaw-bridge
frontmatter.description: Bridge communication between OpenClaw and ZeroClaw AI agents. Enables OpenClaw to dispatch tasks, send messages, and receive responses from ZeroClaw instances via HTTP gateway. Use when integrating with ZeroClaw agents running on VPS or remote systems, dispatching tasks to ZeroClaw for execution, building multi-agent workflows between OpenClaw and ZeroClaw, or checking ZeroClaw gateway status and health.

frontmatter.name: ai-humanizer
frontmatter.description: Transform AI-generated text into natural, human-like writing. Use when the user wants to humanize content, make AI text undetectable, bypass AI detectors, or convert robotic-sounding text into conversational prose. Supports multiple free humanizer services including ai-text-humanizer.com, QuillBot, Grammarly, and HumanizeAI.pro.

frontmatter.name: openclaw-token-optimizer
frontmatter.description: Reduce OpenClaw API costs by 70-95% through smart model routing, local fallbacks, and lean session management. Use when managing API spend, selecting cost-effective models, setting up budget guardrails, configuring Ollama for local fallback, or optimizing token usage across sessions.

frontmatter.name: dscr-real-estate
frontmatter.description: Reference guide for Debt Service Coverage Ratio (DSCR) real estate investment analysis. Use when users need to evaluate residential rental properties for cash flow potential, particularly Section 8 housing investments. Covers market selection, property screening criteria, safety vetting, HUD Fair Market Rent lookups, and DSCR loan calculations.

frontmatter.name: openrouter-free-models-hunter
frontmatter.description: Discover and catalog all free-tier models available on OpenRouter, including their specifications, rate limits, and use case recommendations. Use when looking for zero-cost AI models, comparing free vs paid options, finding budget-friendly alternatives, or researching OpenRouter's free tier offerings.

frontmatter.name: copywriting
frontmatter.description: When the user wants to write, rewrite, or improve marketing copy for any page — including homepage, landing pages, pricing pages, feature pages, about pages, or product pages. Also use when the user says "write copy for," "improve this copy," "rewrite this page," "marketing copy," "headline help," or "CTA copy." For email copy, see email-sequence. For popup copy, see popup-cro.

frontmatter.name: marketing-ideas
frontmatter.description: When the user needs marketing ideas, inspiration, or strategies for their SaaS or software product. Also use when the user asks for 'marketing ideas,' 'growth ideas,' 'how to market,' 'marketing strategies,' 'marketing tactics,' 'ways to promote,' or 'ideas to grow.' This skill provides 139 proven marketing approaches organized by category.

frontmatter.name: onboarding-cro
frontmatter.description: When the user wants to optimize post-signup onboarding, user activation, first-run experience, or time-to-value. Also use when the user mentions "onboarding flow," "activation rate," "user activation," "first-run experience," "empty states," "onboarding checklist," "aha moment," or "new user experience." For signup/registration optimization, see signup-flow-cro. For ongoing email sequences, see email-sequence.

frontmatter.name: programmatic-seo
frontmatter.description: When the user wants to create SEO-driven pages at scale using templates and data. Also use when the user mentions "programmatic SEO," "template pages," "pages at scale," "directory pages," "location pages," "[keyword] + [city] pages," "comparison pages," "integration pages," or "building many pages for SEO." For auditing existing SEO issues, see seo-audit.

frontmatter.name: ai-seo
frontmatter.description: When the user wants to optimize content for AI search engines, get cited by LLMs, or appear in AI-generated answers. Also use when the user mentions 'AI SEO,' 'AEO,' 'GEO,' 'LLMO,' 'answer engine optimization,' 'generative engine optimization,' 'LLM optimization,' 'AI Overviews,' 'optimize for ChatGPT,' 'optimize for Perplexity,' 'AI citations,' 'AI visibility,' or 'zero-click search.' This skill covers content optimization for AI answer engines, monitoring AI visibility, and getting cited as a source. For traditional technical and on-page SEO audits, see seo-audit. For structured data implementation, see schema-markup.

frontmatter.name: ab-test-setup
frontmatter.description: When the user wants to plan, design, or implement an A/B test or experiment. Also use when the user mentions "A/B test," "split test," "experiment," "test this change," "variant copy," "multivariate test," or "hypothesis." For tracking implementation, see analytics-tracking.

frontmatter.name: churn-prevention
frontmatter.description: When the user wants to reduce churn, build cancellation flows, set up save offers, recover failed payments, or implement retention strategies. Also use when the user mentions 'churn,' 'cancel flow,' 'offboarding,' 'save offer,' 'dunning,' 'failed payment recovery,' 'win-back,' 'retention,' 'exit survey,' 'pause subscription,' or 'involuntary churn.' This skill covers voluntary churn (cancel flows, save offers, exit surveys) and involuntary churn (dunning, payment recovery). For post-cancel win-back email sequences, see email-sequence. For in-app upgrade paywalls, see paywall-upgrade-cro.

frontmatter.name: social-content
frontmatter.description: When the user wants help creating, scheduling, or optimizing social media content for LinkedIn, Twitter/X, Instagram, TikTok, Facebook, or other platforms. Also use when the user mentions 'LinkedIn post,' 'Twitter thread,' 'social media,' 'content calendar,' 'social scheduling,' 'engagement,' or 'viral content.' This skill covers content creation, repurposing, and platform-specific strategies.

frontmatter.name: sales-enablement
frontmatter.description: When the user wants to create sales collateral, pitch decks, one-pagers, objection handling docs, or demo scripts. Also use when the user mentions 'sales deck,' 'pitch deck,' 'one-pager,' 'leave-behind,' 'objection handling,' 'ROI calculator,' 'demo script,' 'talk track,' 'sales playbook,' 'proposal template,' or 'buyer persona card.' For competitor battle cards and comparison pages, see competitor-alternatives. For marketing website copy, see copywriting. For cold outreach emails, see cold-email.

frontmatter.name: paid-ads
frontmatter.description: When the user wants help with paid advertising campaigns on Google Ads, Meta (Facebook/Instagram), LinkedIn, Twitter/X, or other ad platforms. Also use when the user mentions 'PPC,' 'paid media,' 'ROAS,' 'CPA,' 'ad campaign,' 'retargeting,' or 'audience targeting.' This skill covers campaign strategy, audience targeting, and optimization. For bulk ad creative generation and iteration, see ad-creative.

frontmatter.name: product-marketing-context
frontmatter.description: When the user wants to create or update their product marketing context document. Also use when the user mentions 'product context,' 'marketing context,' 'set up context,' 'positioning,' or wants to avoid repeating foundational information across marketing tasks. Creates `.agents/product-marketing-context.md` that other marketing skills reference.

frontmatter.name: page-cro
frontmatter.description: When the user wants to optimize, improve, or increase conversions on any marketing page — including homepage, landing pages, pricing pages, feature pages, or blog posts. Also use when the user says "CRO," "conversion rate optimization," "this page isn't converting," "improve conversions," or "why isn't this page working." For signup/registration flows, see signup-flow-cro. For post-signup activation, see onboarding-cro. For forms outside of signup, see form-cro. For popups/modals, see popup-cro.

frontmatter.name: paywall-upgrade-cro
frontmatter.description: When the user wants to create or optimize in-app paywalls, upgrade screens, upsell modals, or feature gates. Also use when the user mentions "paywall," "upgrade screen," "upgrade modal," "upsell," "feature gate," "convert free to paid," "freemium conversion," "trial expiration screen," "limit reached screen," "plan upgrade prompt," or "in-app pricing." Distinct from public pricing pages (see page-cro) — this skill focuses on in-product upgrade moments where the user has already experienced value.

frontmatter.name: launch-strategy
frontmatter.description: When the user wants to plan a product launch, feature announcement, or release strategy. Also use when the user mentions 'launch,' 'Product Hunt,' 'feature release,' 'announcement,' 'go-to-market,' 'beta launch,' 'early access,' 'waitlist,' or 'product update.' This skill covers phased launches, channel strategy, and ongoing launch momentum.

frontmatter.name: site-architecture
frontmatter.description: When the user wants to plan, map, or restructure their website's page hierarchy, navigation, URL structure, or internal linking. Also use when the user mentions "sitemap," "site map," "visual sitemap," "site structure," "page hierarchy," "information architecture," "IA," "navigation design," "URL structure," "breadcrumbs," "internal linking strategy," or "website planning." NOT for XML sitemaps (that's technical SEO — see seo-audit). For SEO audits, see seo-audit. For structured data, see schema-markup.

frontmatter.name: pricing-strategy
frontmatter.description: When the user wants help with pricing decisions, packaging, or monetization strategy. Also use when the user mentions 'pricing,' 'pricing tiers,' 'freemium,' 'free trial,' 'packaging,' 'price increase,' 'value metric,' 'Van Westendorp,' 'willingness to pay,' or 'monetization.' This skill covers pricing research, tier structure, and packaging strategy.

frontmatter.name: form-cro
frontmatter.description: When the user wants to optimize any form that is NOT signup/registration — including lead capture forms, contact forms, demo request forms, application forms, survey forms, or checkout forms. Also use when the user mentions "form optimization," "lead form conversions," "form friction," "form fields," "form completion rate," or "contact form." For signup/registration forms, see signup-flow-cro. For popups containing forms, see popup-cro.

frontmatter.name: ad-creative
frontmatter.description: When the user wants to generate, iterate, or scale ad creative — headlines, descriptions, primary text, or full ad variations — for any paid advertising platform. Also use when the user mentions 'ad copy variations,' 'ad creative,' 'generate headlines,' 'RSA headlines,' 'bulk ad copy,' 'ad iterations,' 'creative testing,' or 'ad performance optimization.' This skill covers generating ad creative at scale, iterating based on performance data, and enforcing platform character limits. For campaign strategy and targeting, see paid-ads. For landing page copy, see copywriting.

frontmatter.name: analytics-tracking
frontmatter.description: When the user wants to set up, improve, or audit analytics tracking and measurement. Also use when the user mentions "set up tracking," "GA4," "Google Analytics," "conversion tracking," "event tracking," "UTM parameters," "tag manager," "GTM," "analytics implementation," or "tracking plan." For A/B test measurement, see ab-test-setup.

frontmatter.name: schema-markup
frontmatter.description: When the user wants to add, fix, or optimize schema markup and structured data on their site. Also use when the user mentions "schema markup," "structured data," "JSON-LD," "rich snippets," "schema.org," "FAQ schema," "product schema," "review schema," or "breadcrumb schema." For broader SEO issues, see seo-audit.

frontmatter.name: referral-program
frontmatter.description: When the user wants to create, optimize, or analyze a referral program, affiliate program, or word-of-mouth strategy. Also use when the user mentions 'referral,' 'affiliate,' 'ambassador,' 'word of mouth,' 'viral loop,' 'refer a friend,' or 'partner program.' This skill covers program design, incentive structure, and growth optimization.

frontmatter.name: cold-email
frontmatter.description: Write B2B cold emails and follow-up sequences that get replies. Use when the user wants to write cold outreach emails, prospecting emails, cold email campaigns, sales development emails, or SDR emails. Covers subject lines, opening lines, body copy, CTAs, personalization, and multi-touch follow-up sequences.

frontmatter.name: copy-editing
frontmatter.description: When the user wants to edit, review, or improve existing marketing copy. Also use when the user mentions 'edit this copy,' 'review my copy,' 'copy feedback,' 'proofread,' 'polish this,' 'make this better,' or 'copy sweep.' This skill provides a systematic approach to editing marketing copy through multiple focused passes.

frontmatter.name: seo-audit
frontmatter.description: When the user wants to audit, review, or diagnose SEO issues on their site. Also use when the user mentions "SEO audit," "technical SEO," "why am I not ranking," "SEO issues," "on-page SEO," "meta tags review," or "SEO health check." For building pages at scale to target keywords, see programmatic-seo. For adding structured data, see schema-markup.

frontmatter.name: free-tool-strategy
frontmatter.description: When the user wants to plan, evaluate, or build a free tool for marketing purposes — lead generation, SEO value, or brand awareness. Also use when the user mentions "engineering as marketing," "free tool," "marketing tool," "calculator," "generator," "interactive tool," "lead gen tool," "build a tool for leads," or "free resource." This skill bridges engineering and marketing — useful for founders and technical marketers.

frontmatter.name: marketing-psychology
frontmatter.description: When the user wants to apply psychological principles, mental models, or behavioral science to marketing. Also use when the user mentions 'psychology,' 'mental models,' 'cognitive bias,' 'persuasion,' 'behavioral science,' 'why people buy,' 'decision-making,' or 'consumer behavior.' This skill provides 70+ mental models organized for marketing application.

frontmatter.name: email-sequence
frontmatter.description: When the user wants to create or optimize an email sequence, drip campaign, automated email flow, or lifecycle email program. Also use when the user mentions "email sequence," "drip campaign," "nurture sequence," "onboarding emails," "welcome sequence," "re-engagement emails," "email automation," or "lifecycle emails." For in-app onboarding, see onboarding-cro.

frontmatter.name: competitor-alternatives
frontmatter.description: When the user wants to create competitor comparison or alternative pages for SEO and sales enablement. Also use when the user mentions 'alternative page,' 'vs page,' 'competitor comparison,' 'comparison page,' '[Product] vs [Product],' '[Product] alternative,' or 'competitive landing pages.' Covers four formats: singular alternative, plural alternatives, you vs competitor, and competitor vs competitor. Emphasizes deep research, modular content architecture, and varied section types beyond feature tables.

frontmatter.name: content-strategy
frontmatter.description: When the user wants to plan a content strategy, decide what content to create, or figure out what topics to cover. Also use when the user mentions "content strategy," "what should I write about," "content ideas," "blog strategy," "topic clusters," or "content planning." For writing individual pieces, see copywriting. For SEO-specific audits, see seo-audit.

frontmatter.name: popup-cro
frontmatter.description: When the user wants to create or optimize popups, modals, overlays, slide-ins, or banners for conversion purposes. Also use when the user mentions "exit intent," "popup conversions," "modal optimization," "lead capture popup," "email popup," "announcement banner," or "overlay." For forms outside of popups, see form-cro. For general page conversion optimization, see page-cro.

frontmatter.name: signup-flow-cro
frontmatter.description: When the user wants to optimize signup, registration, account creation, or trial activation flows. Also use when the user mentions "signup conversions," "registration friction," "signup form optimization," "free trial signup," "reduce signup dropoff," or "account creation flow." For post-signup onboarding, see onboarding-cro. For lead capture forms (not account creation), see form-cro.

frontmatter.name: revops
frontmatter.description: When the user wants help with revenue operations, lead lifecycle management, or marketing-to-sales handoff processes. Also use when the user mentions 'RevOps,' 'revenue operations,' 'lead scoring,' 'lead routing,' 'MQL,' 'SQL,' 'pipeline stages,' 'deal desk,' 'CRM automation,' 'marketing-to-sales handoff,' or 'data hygiene.' For cold outreach emails, see cold-email. For email drip campaigns, see email-sequence. For pricing decisions, see pricing-strategy.
